skill.name: flux-kontext
skill.description: Edit images with Flux 1 Kontext Pro (Black Forest Labs' precise local image-edit model) on RunComfy — bundled with the model's documented prompting patterns so the skill gets sharper output than naive prompting against the same model.
skill.summary: Precise local image editing with Flux 1 Kontext Pro via RunComfy CLI
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: Skill is a legitimate wrapper for the RunComfy CLI that invokes Flux 1 Kontext Pro for image editing. Static analysis detected patterns consistent with CLI command documentation and legitimate API endpoint references. All 53 flagged patterns evaluated as false positives: external_commands are documented CLI invocations, network URLs are RunComfy service endpoints, filesystem references are standard token storage, and cryptographic algorithm detections are scanner artifacts. No confirmed security risks.

content.user_title: Edit Images with Flux 1 Kontext Pro on RunComfy
content.value_statement: Need precise local edits to images while preserving identity and quality? Flux 1 Kontext Pro delivers high-fidelity single-reference edits with strong prompt control, powered by the RunComfy CLI integration.
actual_capabilities: Single-reference precise local image edits with high fidelity preservation
Invokes Flux 1 Kontext Pro model via RunComfy CLI with optimized prompts
Supports seed-based reproducibility for variant comparisons
Handles aspect ratio specification for output format control
Downloads generated outputs to local directory with size cap protection
Documents routing guidance to sibling models (Nano Banana, GPT Image 2, Flux 2 Klein)
limitations: Single source image only — multi-image edits require Nano Banana Edit
Compound prompts may cause drift — split into sequential single-instruction passes
Multilingual or embedded text editing typically better handled by GPT Image 2
Minimum RunComfy CLI setup and authentication required

use_cases.title: Product Photography Brand Edits
use_cases.description: Swap brand text on product labels while preserving bottle silhouette, lighting, and packaging identity. Lead with preservation instruction for consistent high-fidelity results.

use_cases.title: Portrait Composition Updates
use_cases.description: Add props like umbrellas or accessories to portrait photos while preserving face, pose, and clothing. Single declarative instruction format ensures sharp output.

use_cases.title: Iterative Design Refinements
use_cases.description: Make small compositional changes to creative assets using sequential single-instruction passes when complex edits cause model drift. Reproducible via seed parameter.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Prop Addition
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit this image with Flux Kontext: Keep [preserved elements] unchanged. Add [new element with description].
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding simple props like accessories or objects to existing images

prompt_templates.title: Brand Text Replacement
prompt_templates.prompt: Using Flux Kontext, keep the [product shape], [packaging], and [lighting] exactly as in the input. Replace the [text/labels] from '[old value]' to '[new value]' using [font style details].
prompt_templates.scenario: Updating branded content while maintaining visual identity

prompt_templates.title: Style-Consistent Outfit Change
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit with Flux Kontext: Keep [face], [pose], and [framing] unchanged. Change the [clothing item] to [new description with style and color].
prompt_templates.scenario: Swapping clothing or accessories while preserving subject identity

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Pass Complex Edit
prompt_templates.prompt: Step 1 with Flux Kontext: Keep [unchanged elements]. Change [first element]. Then step 2: Keep [all previous elements]. Change [second element].
prompt_templates.scenario: Handling compound edits that require iteration to avoid drift

output_examples.input: A portrait photo of a person standing in a park with a neutral expression
output_examples.output: Edited portrait with an orange umbrella added in the left hand and a slight smile, while preserving the original face, pose, and clothing

output_examples.input: Product photo of a bottle with label text reading 'ALPHA' on a wooden table
output_examples.output: Brand asset with label text updated to 'AURA' in white on black, same font weight, centered, with original bottle silhouette and lighting preserved

best_practices: Lead prompts with preservation instructions: 'Keep [identity/pose/elements] unchanged.' followed by the change specification
Use single declarative instructions per pass — compound prompts cause model drift
Select primary reference image when multiple sources exist — Kontext accepts only single ref
anti_patterns: Compound multi-change prompts like 'change A and add B and remove C' — causes output drift
Passing multiple source images — use Nano Banana Edit for multi-image flows instead
Passive voice phrasing in edit instructions — reduces reliability of changes

faq.question: What makes Flux Kontext different from other image editing models?
faq.answer: Flux Kontext is specifically optimized for single-reference precise local edits with high fidelity preservation. It excels at targeted changes while maintaining source identity.

faq.question: Can I edit multiple images at once with this skill?
faq.answer: No, Flux Kontext accepts only a single source image. For batch editing across 1-20 images, route to Nano Banana Edit instead.

faq.question: Why do my compound edits produce inconsistent results?
faq.answer: Kontext performs best with single declarative instructions. Split compound edits into sequential passes — change one element per pass — to maintain output consistency.

faq.question: How do I reproduce the same edit with variations?
faq.answer: Use the seed parameter when invoking the model. The same seed value produces consistent outputs, allowing you to compare variant results.

faq.question: What should I do if my edit drifts from the source identity?
faq.answer: Add more specific preservation instructions at the start of your prompt. Identify the exact elements to preserve and describe them precisely.

faq.question: When should I use a different Flux model instead of Kontext?
faq.answer: Use Flux 2 Klein for generating images from scratch with no source. Use GPT Image 2 edit for multilingual or embedded text editing. Use Nano Banana Edit for multi-image batch processing.
